supported_claims: OpenCodeReview supports both rule-based checks and large language model (LLM) analysis for comprehensive code review, as stated in the repository's README.
The tool can be integrated into CI/CD pipelines via GitHub Actions, enabling automated review on pull requests.
It provides a plugin system for custom review rules, allowing teams to extend functionality beyond built-in checks.
common_pitfalls: Over-reliance on LLM suggestions without human validation can introduce subtle bugs or security issues.
Misconfiguring rule severity levels may cause noisy reviews that reduce developer trust.
Failing to update review rules as the codebase evolves leads to outdated or irrelevant feedback.
research_questions: How does OpenCodeReview compare to other open-source AI code review tools like CodeRabbit or Amazon CodeGuru Reviewer?
What specific LLM models are supported, and how do they handle context from large pull requests?
What is the performance overhead of running OpenCodeReview in a CI pipeline for large repositories?
success_criteria: Reduction in human review time by at least 30% for routine code changes.
Detection of at least 80% of common coding standard violations before human review.
Positive developer feedback on the relevance and actionability of automated suggestions.
